title: Israel counter-attacks Iran — strikes Mahshahr petrochemical complex (first energy site since April 8 ceasefire) + dismantles restored air defenses; IDF: 'unique materials critical for ballistic missile program'; 15 wounded, 0 killed; Netanyahu defied Trump
snippet: Israeli Air Force struck military targets in western and central Iran overnight (June 8). Dozens of warplanes targeted Mahshahr petrochemical complex in Khuzestan province — the first energy-linked site hit since the April 8 ceasefire. IDF stated the complex produced 'unique materials critical for Iran's ballistic missile program.' Strikes also focused on dismantling restored air defense systems that Iran had been rebuilding since Operation Roaring Lion. 15 wounded (14 from Mahshahr/Khuzestan, 1 from Tehran), no fatalities reported. Netanyahu proceeded with the strikes DESPITE Trump asking him to stop. Covered by NPR, Jerusalem Post (IDF video), Business Standard, Rappler, WDBO/AP, NewsNation/AP.
tag_reason: First Israeli counter-attack on Iranian soil targeting energy infrastructure since April 8 ceasefire; Mahshahr petrochemical is a new target; Netanyahu's defiance of Trump is a new dimension of the US-Israel rift
